notes: Data compiled from web research on 008 Magazine (008magazine.com) — Acadiana's #1 Lifestyle Publication covering travel, events, football, nightlife — and The Independent (theind.com), the alt-weekly covering local news, arts, and culture. Both cover Lafayette/Acadiana. 008 Magazine has 300+ distribution points, 30,000 copies per issue, published every 6 weeks. The Independent covers news, arts, dining, and community events.

festivals.name: Festival International de Louisiane
festivals.description: One of the largest free outdoor international music and arts festivals in the US, celebrating Francophone culture with music, dance, visual arts, food, and crafts. Over 100,000 attendees annually.
festivals.dates: Late April (typically last full weekend of April, Wednesday–Sunday)

festivals.name: Festivals Acadiens et Créoles
festivals.description: Celebration of Cajun and Creole music, food, and crafts. One of the premier Cajun music festivals in the world, held in Girard Park.

festivals.name: Downtown Alive!
festivals.description: Free outdoor concert series in the heart of downtown Lafayette featuring local and regional musicians, food vendors, and a lively street party atmosphere. A beloved Friday evening tradition.
festivals.dates: Friday evenings, Spring (March–May) and Fall (September–November) seasons

festivals.name: Breaux Bridge Crawfish Festival
festivals.description: Annual festival celebrating the crawfish capital of the world, featuring Cajun music, dancing, crawfish eating contests, and authentic Louisiana cuisine.

festivals.name: Boudin Festival / World Championship Boudin Cook-Off
festivals.description: Festival celebrating boudin, the iconic Cajun sausage, with cook-offs, tastings, live music, and family activities. Part of the Scott Boudin Festival.
festivals.dates: Last weekend of March / Early April

festivals.name: Courir de Mardi Gras
festivals.description: Traditional Cajun Mardi Gras run through the countryside, where masked and costumed riders on horseback travel from farm to farm begging for ingredients to make a communal gumbo. Authentic rural Cajun tradition.
festivals.location: Eunice, Church Point, Mamou, and surrounding areas
festivals.dates: Mardi Gras day (Fat Tuesday, February or early March)

festivals.name: Mardi Gras Lafayette
festivals.description: Lafayette's Mardi Gras celebration featuring parades, krewe balls, street parties, and family events throughout the city.

festivals.name: Rhythms on the River
festivals.description: Live music festival along the Vermilion River corridor in downtown Lafayette, celebrating local and regional artists with food and arts vendors.

festivals.name: Festival de Musique Acadienne
festivals.description: Part of Festivals Acadiens et Créoles weekend, dedicated specifically to Cajun music on multiple stages.
festivals.dates: Second weekend of October (same as Festivals Acadiens)

festivals.name: Rayne Frog Festival
festivals.description: Annual festival in the Frog Capital of the World celebrating with frog racing, live Cajun music, food, and family fun.

festivals.name: Opelousas Spice & Music Festival
festivals.description: Celebrating the cultural heritage of Opelousas and Zydeco music, the genre's hometown.

festivals.name: Zydeco Music Festival
festivals.description: Annual festival celebrating Zydeco music in its birthplace with legendary performers and authentic Creole food.

festivals.name: New Iberia Sugar Cane Festival
festivals.description: Celebrating the sugarcane harvest with a parade, queen coronation, carnival rides, live music, and traditional Louisiana food.

festivals.name: Acadiana Food & Wine Festival
festivals.description: Showcase of Louisiana's culinary scene featuring local chefs, restaurants, wineries, and craft beverage producers.

festivals.name: Carencro Pepper Festival
festivals.description: Festival celebrating the hot pepper and Cajun spice culture with cook-offs, music, and local food.

recurringEvents.name: Downtown Alive! Concert Series
recurringEvents.description: Free Friday evening outdoor concerts in downtown Lafayette. Features local and regional bands, food vendors, and a festive atmosphere. Lafayette's signature recurring music event.
recurringEvents.schedule: Friday evenings, 5:30–8:30 PM, Spring (March–May) and Fall (September–November)

recurringEvents.name: Acadiana Farmers Market
recurringEvents.description: Fresh local produce, meats, seafood, baked goods, plants, and artisan crafts from Acadiana farmers and makers.

recurringEvents.name: River Ranch Saturday Farmers Market
recurringEvents.description: Popular farmers market in the upscale River Ranch development featuring local vendors, food, and community gathering.

recurringEvents.name: Third Thursday Art Walk
recurringEvents.description: Monthly art walk through downtown Lafayette's galleries, studios, and creative spaces with openings, receptions, and live demonstrations.
recurringEvents.schedule: Third Thursday of each month, evenings

recurringEvents.name: Vermilionville Living History Museum Events
recurringEvents.description: Regular programming at the living history museum on the banks of the Vermilion River, including Cajun music jams, cooking demonstrations, and cultural events.

recurringEvents.name: Warehouse District Live Music
recurringEvents.description: Recurring live music nights at venues throughout Lafayette's Warehouse District including Blue Moon Saloon, Artmosphere, and others.

recurringEvents.name: Cajun Music Jam Sessions
recurringEvents.description: Informal weekly Cajun music jam sessions open to all skill levels at various venues across Acadiana, a beloved cultural tradition.
recurringEvents.location: Various venues: Randol's, Prejean's, Grant Street Dancehall, Lafayette

recurringEvents.name: Food Truck Fridays
recurringEvents.description: Rotating food truck gathering featuring diverse local food trucks, live music, and family-friendly atmosphere.
recurringEvents.location: Various locations including Moncus Park and Oil Center area, Lafayette

recurringEvents.name: Trivia Nights (Acadiana)
recurringEvents.description: Weekly trivia nights at various bars and restaurants across Lafayette and Acadiana, popular with locals.
recurringEvents.location: Various: Pamplona Tapas Bar, Tsunami, Walk-Ons, and others

recurringEvents.name: Sunday Zydeco Brunch
recurringEvents.description: Live Zydeco music during Sunday brunch at select Acadiana restaurants, a beloved local tradition.

recurringEvents.name: Moncus Park Events
recurringEvents.description: Regular programming at Moncus Park including yoga in the park, dog events, movie nights, and community gatherings.

recurringEvents.name: Oil Center Arts & Gallery Events
recurringEvents.description: Monthly gallery openings and art events in the Oil Center district, home to several independent galleries and studios.

recurringEvents.name: Blue Moon Saloon Concerts
recurringEvents.description: Intimate live music venue hosting Cajun, Zydeco, blues, Americana, and roots music. One of Lafayette's most beloved music venues.
recurringEvents.location: Blue Moon Saloon, 215 E Convent St, Lafayette

featuredBusinesses.name: Blue Moon Saloon
featuredBusinesses.description: Legendary Lafayette music venue and guest house featuring live Cajun, Zydeco, and roots music. A must-visit for authentic Acadiana culture.

featuredBusinesses.name: Prejean's Restaurant
featuredBusinesses.description: Classic Lafayette restaurant serving authentic Cajun cuisine in a setting featuring live Cajun music, dancing, and a mounted alligator. A Lafayette institution since 1980.

featuredBusinesses.name: Randol's Seafood & Restaurant
featuredBusinesses.description: Beloved Lafayette restaurant and Cajun dance hall serving fresh seafood, crawfish, and authentic Cajun plates with live Cajun music and dancing nightly.

featuredBusinesses.name: Artmosphere Bistro
featuredBusinesses.description: Eclectic bistro in downtown Lafayette blending art, live music, and creative cuisine. Gallery walls rotate local artists; hosts regular live performances.

featuredBusinesses.name: Pamplona Tapas Bar
featuredBusinesses.description: Spanish-inspired tapas bar in downtown Lafayette known for creative cocktails, extensive wine list, trivia nights, and lively atmosphere.

featuredBusinesses.name: Grant Street Dancehall
featuredBusinesses.description: Historic Lafayette concert venue and dancehall hosting national touring acts, local bands, Cajun and Zydeco music nights.

featuredBusinesses.name: The Filling Station
featuredBusinesses.description: Popular Lafayette bar and live music venue known for local acts, craft beers, and a laid-back Acadiana atmosphere.

featuredBusinesses.name: Tsunami Sushi
featuredBusinesses.description: Lafayette staple for sushi and Asian fusion, known for creative rolls, lively bar scene, and regular events including trivia nights.

featuredBusinesses.name: Café des Amis
featuredBusinesses.description: Iconic Breaux Bridge restaurant famous for its Saturday Zydeco breakfast with live music and dancing, Cajun and Creole cuisine.
featuredBusinesses.address: 140 E Bridge St, Breaux Bridge, LA 70517

featuredBusinesses.name: Vermilionville
featuredBusinesses.description: Living history museum and folklife park on the banks of the Vermilion River, showcasing Cajun, Creole, and Native American cultures through restored historic buildings, music, and crafts.

featuredBusinesses.name: Acadiana Center for the Arts
featuredBusinesses.description: Primary arts center for the Acadiana region, hosting visual arts exhibitions, performing arts, education programs, and community events.

featuredBusinesses.name: Lafayette Science Museum
featuredBusinesses.description: Science and natural history museum with hands-on exhibits, planetarium, and educational programming for all ages.

featuredBusinesses.name: Mulate's Cajun Restaurant
featuredBusinesses.description: Casual Cajun restaurant and dancehall in Breaux Bridge, one of the original Cajun dancehalls still serving authentic food and live music.

featuredBusinesses.name: Borden's Ice Cream
featuredBusinesses.description: Lafayette's beloved ice cream shop, a family institution serving scoops since 1940. A nostalgic local landmark.

featuredBusinesses.name: Johnson's Boucanière
featuredBusinesses.description: Old-school Cajun smokehouse and deli serving boudin, smoked meats, and andouille. A Lafayette favorite for authentic Cajun charcuterie.

featuredBusinesses.name: The Independent (theind.com / 008 Magazine)
featuredBusinesses.description: Acadiana's alternative weekly newspaper covering local news, arts, culture, food, music, and community events. Essential guide to Lafayette and Acadiana life.

featuredBusinesses.name: Billy's Boudin & Cracklins
featuredBusinesses.description: Iconic Cajun butcher shop and deli known for some of the best boudin and cracklins in all of Acadiana. A must-stop for visitors and locals alike.

featuredBusinesses.name: Rêve Coffee Lab
featuredBusinesses.description: Specialty coffee roaster and café in downtown Lafayette known for high-quality single-origin coffees, pour-overs, and a creative atmosphere.

featuredBusinesses.name: Social Southern Table & Bar
featuredBusinesses.description: Upscale casual restaurant in Lafayette featuring Southern-inspired cuisine, craft cocktails, and a vibrant social atmosphere.

featuredBusinesses.name: The Feed & Seed
featuredBusinesses.description: Eclectic bar and entertainment venue in Lafayette's Freetown neighborhood hosting live music, comedy, art shows, and community events.

featuredBusinesses.name: Deuxième Mardi Gras Krewe Events
featuredBusinesses.description: Various Mardi Gras krewe events, balls, and parades throughout Lafayette's Mardi Gras season.

neighborhoods.name: Downtown Lafayette
neighborhoods.description: The vibrant cultural and entertainment heart of Lafayette, home to Parc Sans Souci, the Acadiana Center for the Arts, Lafayette Science Museum, boutique restaurants, bars, music venues, and the weekly Downtown Alive! concert series.

neighborhoods.name: Freetown
neighborhoods.description: One of Lafayette's historically African American neighborhoods, now an emerging arts and culture district with deep roots in Zydeco music, soul food, and community heritage. Home to creative spaces and local venues.

neighborhoods.name: Oil Center
neighborhoods.description: Lafayette's first planned commercial district, developed during the oil boom era. Now home to upscale restaurants, professional offices, boutique shops, galleries, and a mature tree-lined ambiance.

neighborhoods.name: River Ranch
neighborhoods.description: Planned upscale residential and retail community along the Vermilion River with luxury townhomes, fine dining, boutique shops, a popular farmers market, and scenic waterfront.

neighborhoods.name: Johnston Street Corridor
neighborhoods.description: Lafayette's major commercial corridor stretching from downtown toward the university area, lined with restaurants, shops, entertainment venues, and Moncus Park.

neighborhoods.name: Breaux Bridge
neighborhoods.description: Small town on the Bayou Teche just east of Lafayette, known as the Crawfish Capital of the World. Famous for Café des Amis Zydeco breakfast, Mulate's dancehall, antique shops, and the annual Crawfish Festival.

neighborhoods.name: Scott
neighborhoods.description: Town just west of Lafayette known as a hub for authentic boudin and Cajun cuisine, home to the annual Scott Boudin Festival and numerous boudin shops.

neighborhoods.name: Youngsville
neighborhoods.description: Rapidly growing suburban city south of Lafayette, known for family-friendly neighborhoods, Sugar Mill Pond development, new restaurants, and outdoor recreation.

neighborhoods.name: Broussard
neighborhoods.description: Growing community south of Lafayette with a mix of residential neighborhoods, local restaurants, and community events. Home to a strong Cajun community.

neighborhoods.name: Eunice
neighborhoods.description: Prairie Cajun town in St. Landry Parish known as a center for Cajun music and Courir de Mardi Gras. Home to the Cajun Music Hall of Fame, Liberty Theater, and authentic rural Cajun culture.

neighborhoods.name: Opelousas
neighborhoods.description: Parish seat of St. Landry Parish, considered the Zydeco music capital of the world and birthplace of the genre. Rich in African American Creole culture, music history, and antebellum architecture.

neighborhoods.name: St. Martinville
neighborhoods.description: Historic town on the Bayou Teche, deeply tied to Cajun and Acadian heritage. Home of the Evangeline Oak, the Longfellow-Evangeline State Historic Site, and a beautifully preserved historic downtown.
highlights: Historic St. Martin de Tours Church
